summary: Completed the matching C decompilation of Ness's ftNess_7 module, replacing the large ftNess_7.s assembly object with src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_7.c in obj_files.mk. The new C covers Ness PK Thunder / PK Thunder 2 action-state handlers, animation callbacks, physics callbacks, collision callbacks, projectile ownership cleanup, GFX cleanup, and angle/velocity calculations. The PR also expanded Ness headers and fighter special-attribute typing needed by the module, made a small CollData field-name fix in Pikachu code, and included a minor whitespace cleanup in include/math.h. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided dump, so motivations beyond the title and diff are inferred from the matching decomp workflow.
what_changed: Removed almost all of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_7.s, which previously contained about 2121 lines of handwritten/disassembled PowerPC for ftNess_7.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_7.c with about 1830 lines of matching C for functions in the 0x80117B70 through 0x80119D58 address range.
Changed obj_files.mk so the build uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_7.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_7.s.o.
Implemented Ness PK Thunder-related logic, including startup/control/end/PK Thunder 2 action-state handlers and animation, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks.
Added helper return_float* functions and comments labeled 'Setup float order' to preserve float/double constant ordering for matching.
Added local inline helpers such as fabs_inline_ness and check_distance to match distance checks against PK Thunder position data using thresholds 8.333333015441895f and 12.333333015441895f.
Used sa.ness.x2244 and sa.ness.x224C to track Ness's PK Thunder object pointer/state and related GFX cleanup, including calls to efLib_DestroyAll and func_802AB9C0.
Expanded ftNess.h with a larger ftNessAttributes definition, PK Flash / PK Fire / PK Thunder / PSI Magnet / Yo-yo attribute field names, and many ftNess function prototypes.
Updated fighter.h SpecialAttrs_Ness so x2244 is typed as void* in the new version, matching the way ftNess_7.c passes it to projectile/item-style helper functions.
Fixed a nearby CollData naming use in ftPikachu/ftpikachu4.c from collData->x190 to collData->x190_vec.
Made a trivial formatting cleanup in include/math.h by changing 'return x ;' to 'return x;'.
smart_moves: The PR performed the standard decomp completion step of switching obj_files.mk from the asm object to the C object only after adding the matching C module.

It retained address-based func_/lbl_ names while adding semantic comments such as 'Ness's grounded PK Thunder Start Action State handler' and 'Ness's PK Thunder 2 Wall Rebound Collision callback', preserving linker identity while documenting behavior.

The float-order helper functions explicitly separated f32 and f64 constants, including notes about problematic 8-byte constants, which is useful when matching sdata ordering and literal pool behavior.
The use of #pragma dont_inline around func_80117B70 and explicit inline helpers for fabs/check_distance shows matching-oriented control over inlining rather than purely idiomatic C.

The decomp reused common fighter engine calls and data paths visible in the assembly: Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, func_8007592C, func_80081298, func_800822A4, lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis, lbvector_Angle, lbvector_Mirror, cosf, sinf, and func_someCalcAngle_80022C30.

The PR captured projectile ownership and cleanup paths: if Ness's stored PK Thunder pointer is null or no longer owned by the fighter, the code transitions to the appropriate end state and clears sa.ness.x2244.
The CollData field rename in Pikachu suggests the header/struct naming changes were validated against at least one other fighter user, not only Ness.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter special-move modules, a matching PR often needs coordinated changes across the C source, character header, shared Fighter struct/header, and obj_files.mk.

When decompiling code that is sensitive to float constants, preserve exact f32 versus f64 literals and constant order; helper functions may be used as a matching tactic even if they are not idiomatic gameplay code.
Action-state IDs may remain numeric in early matching work; this PR uses ranges/cases such as 0x166 through 0x16E for Ness special states while comments explain the PK Thunder state meanings.

Special-attribute fields should be typed according to actual use. In this slice, Ness's x2244 is used as an object pointer passed to PK Thunder helper functions, so typing it as void* in SpecialAttrs_Ness is more useful than an integer.
Decompilation can reveal shared struct naming issues outside the target file; here, the CollData field x190_vec needed to be used in ftPikachu as well.

If matching code contains repeated assignments or filler arrays, do not aggressively clean them up without checking codegen; this ftNess_7.c uses filler arrays and repeated zeroing patterns to match stack layout and instruction order.
Preserve external function prototypes locally when a full header declaration is not yet available, as seen with efLib_DestroyAll and func_802AB3F0.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions and callbacks keep address-based names such as func_80117B70, func_80117BBC, lbl_801186B0, and lbl_80119D58.
Semantic comments are appended to address-based functions, e.g. 'Ness's grounded PK Thunder Control Loop Animation callback' and 'Ness's aerial PK Thunder 2 Physics callback'.
Ness special attributes use offset-prefixed names with descriptive uppercase suffixes, such as x54_PK_THUNDER_2_MOMENTUM, x60_PK_THUNDER_2_KNOCKDOWN_ANGLE, and x64_PK_THUNDER_2_WALLHUG_ANGLE.
Fighter state variables retain offset/stateVar-style names, including x2340_stateVar1, x234C_stateVar4, x2350_stateVar5, x2368_f32, and x2370_f32.
Character special runtime fields are accessed through the fighter union path sa.ness, for example sa.ness.x2244 and sa.ness.x224C.
Collision vector fields use explicit vector suffixes such as x190_vec, as reinforced by the Pikachu one-line fix.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the ftNess_7.s object with a C object in obj_files.mk, the key build-system signal that the module now matches.

Added return_float1 through return_float20-style helpers to force literal ordering and types for constants including 5.0f, 0.0f, 8.333333015441895f, 12.333333015441895f, 6.283185307179586, 3.141592653589793, and 1.5707963267948966.
Used #pragma dont_inline on/off around at least one function to control generated code shape.
Used local inline functions for fabs-like behavior and distance checking rather than calling a library function, matching branch and negation patterns from the assembly.
Preserved explicit f64 casts in angle-normalization loops to match comparisons and arithmetic against double constants such as 2*pi and pi.
Used stack filler arrays in functions such as func_80117BBC to influence local variable layout.
Kept numeric action-state IDs and switch/case shapes where they correspond to assembly compare/range behavior.

Matched vector and angle math through engine helper calls rather than reimplementing them: func_someCalcAngle_80022C30, lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis, lbvector_Angle, lbvector_AngleXY, lbvector_Mirror, cosf, and sinf.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Because no human review text was available, there is no evidence of requested changes, reviewer rationale, or naming feedback beyond what appears in the diff.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftNess_7 functions from address-based func_/lbl_ names to semantic PK Thunder names.
Search for subsequent changes to SpecialAttrs_Ness x2244/x224C typing or names to confirm whether x2244 became a PK Thunder GObj pointer.
Search for later cleanup of ftNess_7 return_float helpers, filler arrays, or repeated assignments after matching constraints changed.
Search for other PRs that changed CollData x190 to x190_vec to understand the broader struct-header migration.
Compare this ftNess_7.c against later ftNess PK Thunder files to see which comments and field names were retained or corrected.
